document.title: Experience of the Usage of a Portable X-ray System

sourceSummary.oneLine: Original research describing temporary clinical use of the FUJIFILM CALNEO Xair portable X-ray system by doctor helicopter staff in Japan.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Literature evidence for clinical experience with a portable X-ray system in a prehospital physician-staffed helicopter setting.
keyPoints: FUJIFILM developed the CALNEO Xair portable X-ray system.
The X-ray radiation machine weighs 3.5 kg and can be transported by ambulance or helicopter.
The study period was January 11 to 18, 2019, during temporary access to the CALNEO Xair system.
Thirteen subjects were classified into the X-ray group and 17 into the control group.
Further studies were needed to determine indications for portable X-ray systems in the prehospital setting.

evidence.claim: The source is original research titled "Experience of the Usage of a Portable X-ray System."
evidence.evidenceText:
Original Research
Experience of the Usage of a Portable X-ray System
evidence.pageTarget: Experience of the Usage of a Portable X-ray System

evidence.claim: FUJIFILM developed a portable X-ray system called the CALNEO Xair.
evidence.evidenceText: FUJIFILM (Tokyo, Japan) developed a portable X-ray system called the CALNEO Xair.

evidence.claim: The CALNEO Xair X-ray radiation machine weighs 3.5 kg.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray radiation machine weighs 3.5 kg (Fig. 1).

evidence.claim: The system can be transported by ambulance or helicopter.
evidence.evidenceText: This system can be transported by an ambulance or helicopter.

evidence.claim: One physician can perform an X-ray examination at the scene and check the image on a computer display.
evidence.evidenceText: Only 1 physician can perform an X-ray examination at the scene and check the image on the computer display, which is transmitted electronically after the X-ray examination

evidence.claim: The retrospective study protocol was approved by the review board of the hospital.
evidence.evidenceText: The protocol of this retrospective study was approved by the review board of our hospital

evidence.claim: The examinations were conducted in accordance with good clinical practice and the Declaration of Helsinki.
evidence.evidenceText: all examinations were conducted in accordance with the standards of good clinical practice and the Declaration of Helsinki.

evidence.claim: Temporary access to the CALNEO Xair system occurred from January 11, 2019, to January 18, 2019.
evidence.evidenceText: From January 11, 2019, to January 18, 2019, we had temporary access to a CALNEO Xair system.

evidence.claim: The study included 28 dispatches with 31 patients after two dispatch cancellations were excluded.
evidence.evidenceText: After excluding these 2 dispatch cancellations, the remaining 28 dispatches with 31 patients were included in this study.

evidence.claim: Thirteen subjects were in the X-ray group and 17 were in the control group.
evidence.evidenceText: Thirteen subjects were classified into the X-ray group, and 17 were classified into the control group.

evidence.claim: No X-ray studies were performed for patients who underwent interhospital transportation.
evidence.evidenceText: The present study shows that no X-ray studies were performed for patients who underwent interhospital transportation

evidence.claim: The authors concluded that further studies are needed to determine indications for portable X-ray systems in the prehospital setting.
evidence.evidenceText: Further studies are needed to determine the indications for using portable X-ray systems in the prehospital setting.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority is identified in the source text; metadata authoritySignal indicates FDA, but FDA is not mentioned in the document.

The source is a journal article and not an official regulatory authority document.
The document date is represented only by publication year 2019 and study/event dates; no exact publication date is provided.
Some table values appear potentially misaligned or OCR-degraded, including group counts and scene/interhospital transportation values in Table 2.
